2603.28378.title: Membership Inference Attacks against Large Audio Language Models
2603.28378.paper_summary: We present the first systematic Membership Inference Attack (MIA) evaluation of Large Audio Language Models (LALMs). As audio encodes non-semantic information, it induces severe train and test distribution shifts and can lead to spurious MIA performance. Using a multi-modal blind baseline based on textual, spectral, and prosodic features, we demonstrate that common speech datasets exhibit near-perfect train/test separability (AUC approximately 1.0) even without model inference, and the standard MIA scores strongly correlate with these blind acoustic artifacts (correlation greater than 0.7). Using this blind baseline, we identify that distribution-matched datasets enable reliable MIA evaluation without distribution shift confounds. We benchmark multiple MIA methods and conduct modality disentanglement experiments on these datasets. The results reveal that LALM memorization is cross-modal, arising only from binding a speaker's vocal identity with its text. These findings establish a principled standard for auditing LALMs beyond spurious correlations.
2603.28378.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了對大型音頻語言模型（LALMs）的首次系統性會員推斷攻擊（MIA）評估。由於音頻編碼了非語義信息，這會引起嚴重的訓練和測試分佈轉變，並可能導致虛假的MIA性能。使用基於文本、頻譜和韻律特徵的多模態盲基準，我們展示了常見的語音數據集即使在沒有模型推斷的情況下也展現出近乎完美的訓練/測試可分性（AUC約為1.0），並且標準的MIA分數與這些盲音頻特徵強烈相關（相關性大於0.7）。利用這個盲基準，我們確定了分佈匹配的數據集能夠在沒有分佈轉變混淆的情況下進行可靠的MIA評估。我們基準測試了多種MIA方法，並在這些數據集上進行了模態解耦實驗。結果顯示，LALM的記憶是跨模態的，僅僅源於將說話者的聲音身份與其文本綁定在一起。這些發現為審計LALMs建立了一個有原則的標準，超越了虛假的相關性。
2603.28378.author: Jia-Kai Dong et.al.
2603.28378.authors: Jia-Kai Dong, Yu-Xiang Lin, Hung-Yi Lee
